science.title: Breakthrough discovery reveals hidden oxygen flow deep inside catalysts
science.summary: A major discovery is reshaping how scientists think about catalysts. Researchers have, for the first time, captured oxygen atoms moving through the interior of a catalyst—not just along its surface. This reveals that the bulk material can actively participate in reactions, opening a new frontier in catalyst design. The

science.title: This donut-shaped discovery just shattered a 150-year math rule
science.summary: A 150-year-old rule in geometry has been proven wrong. Mathematicians found two different doughnut-shaped surfaces that look identical when measured locally but are actually different overall. For decades, researchers suspected this might be possible but couldn’t prove it—until now. The breakthrough reshapes how mathem

science.title: This new camera captures what happens in a trillionth of a second
science.summary: Scientists have unveiled a breakthrough imaging method that can capture the hidden details of events unfolding in trillionths of a second. This new technique doesn’t just track how bright something is—it also reveals subtle structural changes that were previously invisible, all in a single shot. By effectively turning

science.title: These tiny dinosaur fossils fooled scientists for 20 years
science.summary: Tiny dinosaur fossils that puzzled scientists for over 20 years have finally revealed their true identity. Rather than belonging to a miniature species, they are actually baby ankylosaurs—some less than a year old, including a possible hatchling. By studying bone growth patterns, researchers confirmed these young dinos

science.title: DNA reveals a hidden pitviper species in China
science.summary: A vivid green pitviper hiding in Sichuan’s misty mountains has been revealed as a completely new species. Scientists had overlooked it for decades, assuming it was a common snake—until DNA analysis proved otherwise. Named after Laozi, it features striking differences between males and females, including bold stripes an

tech.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
tech.summary: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

tech.title: Meta is revamping its cross-app management system
tech.summary: Meta's Accounts Center will transition to the Meta Account, which is supposed to make it easier to manage your experience on various Meta apps and devices.

tech.title: Labor unrest at Samsung may worsen memory chip supply issues
tech.summary: Tens of thousands of Samsung Electronics workers held a rally at the company’s Pyeongtaek campus in South Korea on Thursday to signal they are prepared to walk off the job for an 18-day strike next month.

tech.title: Newly Deciphered Sabotage Malware May Have Targeted Iran’s Nuclear Program—and Predates Stuxnet
tech.summary: Researchers have finally cracked Fast16, mysterious code capable of silently tampering with calculation and simulation software. It was created in 2005—and likely deployed by the US or an ally.

tech.title: The Best Robotic Pool Cleaners of 2026: Beatbot, iGarden, Dreame
tech.summary: Send the pool guy packing. One of these robotic buddies can maintain your water quality instead.

medicine.title: What a 'post‑antibiotic era' could mean for modern medicine
medicine.summary: Antibiotics are one of the greatest breakthroughs in medical history. They turned once-deadly infections into treatable illnesses and made modern health care possible. But bacteria are changing, and some of the drugs we have depended on for decades are becoming less effective.

medicine.title: Simple 'gut reset' procedure may prevent weight rebound following GLP-1 discontinuation
medicine.summary: An outpatient procedure may offer a way for the estimated 70% of people who discontinue popular weight-loss drugs to avoid regaining the pounds they shed, according to a study presented at Digestive Disease Week (DDW) 2026.

medicine.title: How a gentler stem cell transplant may move type 1 diabetes treatment closer
medicine.summary: A combination blood stem cell and pancreatic islet cell transplant from an immunologically mismatched donor completely prevented or cured type 1 diabetes in mice in a study by Stanford Medicine researchers. Type 1 diabetes arises when the immune system mistakenly destroys insulin-producing islet cells in the pancreas.

medicine.title: Simple “gut reset” may stop weight gain after Ozempic or Wegovy
medicine.summary: A new minimally invasive procedure may help people keep weight off after stopping popular drugs like Ozempic and semaglutide—something most patients struggle with. In a clinical trial, those who underwent a technique called duodenal mucosal resurfacing regained far less weight compared to others after discontinuing the

medicine.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
medicine.summary: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. The research suggests that the interaction between bacteria and the viruses they carry may be key to

mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. The post In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere
mathematics.summary: The central limit theorem started as a bar trick for 18th-century gamblers. Now scientists rely on it every day. The post The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

mathematics.title: Laurent Demanet appointed co-director of MIT Center for Computational Science and Engineering
mathematics.summary: Applied mathematics professor will join fellow co-director Nicolas Hadjiconstantinou in leading the cross-cutting center.

space.title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
space.summary: On every crewed mission, NASA packs pouches of a potentially life-saving liquid in its cargo, known as IV (or intravenous) fluid. A simple mix of sodium chloride and purified water, it can treat up to 30% of medical conditions in flight, resolving things like dehydration, burns, and more. Crewed missions beyond low Ear

space.title: Japan's audacious sample-return mission to the Mars moon Phobos has made it to the launch pad
space.summary: The complex MMX mission aims to shed light on the origins of Mars' two tiny moons, Phobos and Deimos, bringing Phobos material back to Earth in 2031.

space.title: Rocket Lab launches Japanese 'origami' satellite, 7 other spacecraft to orbit (photos)
space.summary: Rocket Lab launched eight Japanese satellites to orbit on Wednesday night (April 22), including one that features an origami-folded antenna.

space.title: Celebrate Earth Day with this Artemis 2 shot | Space photo of the day for April 22, 2026
space.summary: Celebrating Earth Day with an image of Earth captured by NASA's Artemis 2 mission around the moon.

space.title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
space.summary: NASA Force aims to recruit industry talent to the space agency for relatively short stints. Applications are open now in the wake of the Artemis 2 moon mission.

astronomy.title: NASA’s SPHEREx Telescope Just Mapped the Cosmic Ices That Will Someday Build Planets
astronomy.summary: New missions mean new capabilities - and one particularly interesting new mission is finally up and running. Data is starting to come in from SPHEREx, the medium-class surveyor that is mapping the entire sky every six months. A paper based on some of that early data was recently published in The Astrophysical Journal,

astronomy.title: Planets Collide Around Young, Sun-like Star
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have uncovered evidence that two planets collided around a young star, revealing how giant impacts sculpt baby solar systems. The post Planets Collide Around Young, Sun-like Star appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Two Worlds Where the Sun Never Moves
astronomy.summary: One side is scorched to over 200 degrees, while the other is plunged into a darkness so cold it falls below minus 200. Welcome to TRAPPIST-1b and 1c, two rocky worlds that have just revealed the first ever climate maps of Earth sized planets beyond our Solar System. The James Webb Space Telescope has been watching, and

astronomy.title: James Webb Space Telescope peers into a dying star surrounded by mysterious buckyballs: 'The structures we're seeing now are breathtaking'
astronomy.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope captured the first detailed images of planetary nebula Tc 1, revealing new details of what happens after a sun-like star dies.

astronomy.title: Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have located the edge of the Milky Way’s star-forming disk for the first time, showing that star formation is focused within 40,000 light-years of our galactic center. The post Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.title: Building Lightness Through Glass and Frames
architecture.summary: Throughout much of history, weight has been closely associated with the very idea of architecture. architecture.title: Gensler set to design Trump Tower Tbilisi as country's tallest building
architecture.summary: The Trump Organization has announced plans to build a 70-storey skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia, which would be the tallest building in the country. architecture.title: Five Texas cities "catalyzing" urban development with convention centres
architecture.summary: Nearly $9 billion of convention centre design and construction is currently underway in Texas's five biggest cities. Local writer Kate Mazade asks experts about the phenomenon and dives into the plans for each city. architecture.title: Flower-covered theatre forms centrepiece of Denise Scott Brown's photography exhibition
architecture.summary: A flower-covered theatre has been installed at the centre of an exhibition dedicated to architect Denise Scott Brown's photographs at the brutalist Yale architecture school building in New Haven, USA. architecture.title: ZGF wraps space shuttle in stainless-steel tower for California Science Center
architecture.summary: American architecture studio ZGF's building in Los Angeles is complete with a full-size space shuttle "in launch position" wrapped in a stainless steel cylinder. construction.title: Costain using 3D printed concrete for Teesside carbon capture project
construction.summary: Infrastructure solutions company Costain, and civil and structural engineering delivery firm A E Yates have brought on board Hyperion Robotics to deliver low-carbon concrete sleepers for a “landmark” East Coast Cluster project on Teesside. construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.summary: Pagabo has launched a £4.16bn civil engineering and enabling works framework - rising to £5bn including VAT - that will consolidate two major national agreements into a single procurement vehicle spanning the UK. construction.title: A422 overbridge construction methodology changes demand extensive temporary works and multi-party collaboration
construction.summary: With the High Speed 2 (HS2) rail line route intercepting a section of the A422 road between Brackley and Buckingham, a new overbridge is being constructed to allow the trains to run underneath the motorway. ai.summary: arXiv:2604.19278v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based multi-agent systems (MAS) show promise on complex tasks but remain prone to coordination failures such as goal drift, error cascades, and misaligned behaviors.
